item.title: South Korea’s Lee Faces First Election Test One Year Into Office
item.description: South Korean President Lee Jae Myung faces his first electoral test a year into his term with local votes that could boost his party’s influence throughout the nation or show the limitations of his appeal as the main opposition party tries to rebuild itself.

item.title: Asian Stocks Poised to Gain as AI Rally Extends: Markets Wrap
item.description: Asian stocks were set to track Wall Street higher as renewed enthusiasm for the artificial-intelligence trade drove the S&P 500 to a ninth day of gains. Oil steadied as conflicting signals clouded the outlook for a US-Iran deal.

item.title: Oil Holds Two-Day Gain on Discordant US-Iran Peace Talk Reports
item.description: Oil steadied after rising in the previous two sessions, as contradictory headlines on the prospects of a US-Iran peace deal boost price swings.

item.title: SpaceX to Plan Setting IPO Terms as Soon as Wednesday
item.description: SpaceX is planning to set the terms of its initial public offering as early as Wednesday afternoon, according to people familiar with the matter, ahead of what’s expected to be the biggest ever listing.

item.title: GameStop Posted Its Highest Profit Ever By Selling Action Figures and Cards
item.description: GameStop Corp. shares climbed as much as 13% in after-hours trading after the video-game retailer reported a record quarterly profit driven by its collectibles business.

item.title: How China’s Wealthy Sidestep Strict Rules to Get Money Out of the Country
item.description: China’s capital controls remain among the world’s strictest. Individuals are generally limited to transferring $50,000 overseas each year, while emigrants are given a one-time opportunity to move their assets abroad.

item.title: Toilet Maker Toto Hikes Capex to Meet AI Demand for Its Ceramics
item.description: Japanese toilet maker Toto Ltd. expects spending in its chip-related operations to make up more than half its total capex in coming years, as it chases new gains from an artificial intelligence surge.

item.title: QXO’s $3 Billion Junk Bond Sale for Takeover Draws Strong Demand
item.description: QXO Building Products Inc.’s $3 billion junk-bond offering to fund its purchase of rival TopBuild Corp. has received more than triple the amount in demand from investors.

item.title: Musk Allies Back Ex-DOGE Staffers Trying to Use AI to Cut Waste
item.description: Two former staffers from the Department of Government Efficiency have unveiled a new venture to buy companies and cut waste by implementing artificial intelligence, applying DOGE’s cost-cutting project to the private sector.

item.title: Huge AI Bonuses Spark South Korea Tech Wealth Fight
item.description: Samsung averted a crippling strike by paying massive bonuses to its chip workers. But the move has sparked resentment in other divisions, fueling a global debate about how to share AI windfalls.

item.title: Short-Sellers Are Dug In as Bond Market Faces Big Payrolls Test
item.description: Bond traders are locked in to wagers on higher yields even after paring some of their more extreme positions, with critical US employment data due on Friday that may add to the argument for Federal Reserve interest-rate hikes.

item.title: Palo Alto Networks Raises Profit Outlook, Citing AI Demand
item.description: Palo Alto Networks Inc. released a forecast for adjusted earnings that was stronger than anticipated, signaling ongoing demand for security services as artificial intelligence-related threats continue to raise concerns for companies and governments.

item.title: Prosus Gets Until Oct. 11 for Delivery Hero Sale After Uber Bid
item.description: The European Commission is planning to extend a critical deadline for Prosus NV to sell down a stake in Delivery Hero SE to October, buying the investor some meaningful time to thwart another possible takeover attempt of the food delivery service by Uber Technologies Inc.

item.title: CoreWeave-Tied Data Center Raises $900 Million in Junk-Bond Sale
item.description: A data center tied to CoreWeave Inc. raised $900 million from a high-yield note offering, joining a wave of junk issuers tapping debt markets to fund artificial intelligence infrastructure.

item.title: Venezuela Moves to Open Power Sector to Private Investment
item.description: Venezuela’s National Assembly will debate a reform that would open the country’s electricity sector to private investment, allowing companies to generate, distribute and sell power under government concessions after more than 15 years of state control.

item.title: Cliffwater Private Credit Fund Stung by 17% Redemption Requests
item.description: Cliffwater LLC’s flagship private credit fund capped redemptions at 5% in the second quarter after investors looked to pull about 17% of shares, in a sign of enduring pressure on the $1.8 trillion market.

item.title: Traders Most Bullish Yuan in 15 Years on Global Role, Valuation
item.description: Traders ramped up wagers on a stronger yuan in the coming year, betting that increased global usage and an attractive valuation will guide China’s currency through near-term speed bumps.

item.title: Petrochemicals Giant Braskem Seeks Support to Start Out-of-Court Rework
item.description: Braskem SA is seeking backing from creditors to begin an out-of-court restructuring process ahead of debt payments scheduled for July, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Colombia’s Pro-Trump Presidential Frontrunner Thrills Investors
item.description: Exuberant market reaction to a celebrity lawyer’s surprise victory in Colombia’s first-round election Sunday is sowing expectations for an investment rebound in the resource-rich Andean country.

item.title: Microsoft’s New Quantum Chip Aiming for Useful Machine in 2029
item.description: Microsoft Corp. announced an updated quantum-computing chip, betting it can set up a commercially useful machine by 2029.

item.title: Microsoft Launches AI That Works Like an Executive Assistant
item.description: Microsoft Corp. launched new artificial intelligence software designed to function like an always-active executive assistant, the latest evolution of its workplace AI efforts.

item.title: Microsoft’s Project Solara Aims to Put AI in an Employee Badge
item.description: Microsoft Corp. introduced a new set of software tools designed to take artificial intelligence agents off of laptop screens and place them in mobile devices for businesses.

item.title: 12 Reasons This Is The Worst Crypto Winter Ever
item.description: It’s getting colder and colder.

item.title: Indie Video Game ‘Mina the Hollower’ Sells 300,000 Copies in Three Days
item.description: The new action-platformer is the best-reviewed video game of 2026 so far

item.title: Canadian Stocks Index Hits 35,000 Amid Rally in Energy
item.description: The Canadian equities benchmark passed 35,000 for the first time on a broadening rally in energy and financials.

item.title: Uber Caps Usage of AI Tools Like Claude Code to Cut Costs
item.description: Uber Technologies Inc. has set usage caps on some artificial intelligence-powered tools used by its staff, a move meant to manage costs after the company blew through its AI budget earlier this year.

item.title: Perplexity Splits AI Work Between PCs and Servers to Ease Strain
item.description: Perplexity AI Inc. is trying to manage the huge demand for artificial intelligence computing power by building a platform that diverts AI work between personal computers and cloud-based servers.

item.title: EU’s AI Data Center Plans Stumble Due to Delays, Funding Issues
item.description: The European Union’s €20 billion ($23.3 billion) investment plan for five massive artificial intelligence data centers is floundering, with delays and funding issues alienating some potential partners, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Alphabet $80 Billion Equity Sale Set to Break Fundraising Record
item.description: Alphabet Inc.’s surprise $80 billion sale of equity to fund spending on artificial intelligence infrastructure is expected to become the biggest equity capital markets transaction of all time, according to data compiled by Bloomberg.

item.title: Santander Employee Arrested in $85 Million Chile Crime Sting
item.description: An employee of Banco Santander’s Chile unit was among 18 suspects detained by police Tuesday as authorities moved against the Venezuelan Tren de Aragua criminal organization allegedly operating in the South American country.
